tossups.question: In an aria about one of these objects, one character notes, "why did fate / Ever decree we two should meet!" as he stands accused of blasphemy. In Act IV of Adriana Lecouvreur ("leku-VROR"), the poisoned Adriana throws some of these objects into the fire before Maurizio embraces her. A character sings about these objects that "already cast their shadow" in a B major aria beginning ("read slowly") "D-sharp, C-sharp, D-sharp, E." The English horn plays a "fate motif" before an aria titled for one of these objects in which Don José sings of a "dry and faded" one in a jail cell. They're not bells, but the maid Mallika and the daughter of a Brahmin priest sing a duet titled for these objects. A "Song" from Carmen is titled after, for 10 points, what objects that, in a duet from Leo Delibes's Lakmé, "bank in bloom"?

A "Song" from Carmen is titled after, for 10 points, what objects that, in a duet from Leo Delibes's Lakme, "bank in bloom"?

tossups.question: One ballet by this composer prominently uses solo alto saxophone in its third act’s barcarolle. In that ballet, the title character shoots a boy with an arrow that was intended for the god Eros. In a song from an opera by this composer, The G major B section contrasts with a (read slowly) B A-sharp B A-sharp G-sharp (pause) A-sharp B C-sharp ostinato in the A section. Another of this composer’s ballets features “The Waltz of the (*) Hours” in the third act before Franz and Swanhilda’s wedding. “Sous le dôme épais” is sung by the servant Mallika and the title daughter of a brahmin priest, who later sings the “Bell Song,” in the most famous piece by this composer of the ballets Sylvia and Coppelia. For 10 points, name this French composer whose opera Lakmé includes the “Flower Duet.”

"Sous le dome epais" is sung by the servant Mallika and the title daughter of a brahmin priest, who later sings the "Bell Song," in the most famous piece by this composer of the ballets Sylvia and Coppelia. For 10 points, name this French composer whose opera Lakme includes the "Flower Duet."

tossups.question: Manon Lescaut’s finale draws on a string quartet named for these objects composed by Giacomo Puccini for the funeral of the Duke of Aosta. After opening a package of these objects, the title character describes them as “poor” and throws some into the fireplace in the opera Adriana Lecouvreur. Above a flowing sixteenth-note accompaniment, the melody (read slowly): long D-sharp, C-sharp – D-sharp – E, opens a song describing a (*) “thick dome” and “dense canopy” of these objects. In Lillias Pastia’s inn, Don José sings an aria to Carmen about one of these objects that she had given him during her “Habanera.” In another opera, the servant Mallika and the title priestess sing a song about these objects as they collect them near a stream. For 10 points, what objects name a celebrated duet from Leo Delibes’s opera Lakmé?
tossups.answer_sanitized: flowers [accept chrysanthemums; accept violets; accept jasmine]

tossups.question: It's not Lakmé, but during a Flower Duet in this opera, the soprano and her mezzo servant pluck every flower in the garden to decorate a house overlooking the harbor. In Act 1 of this opera, relatives at a wedding shout "Hou!" at the protagonist for her conversion at the Mission. The title 15-year-old soprano in this opera says that her lover will call her "little wife" and "scent of verbena" as she waits for a thread of smoke from a naval ship in "Un bel di, vedremo." That aria is delivered to allay Suzuki's doubts by a woman who calls herself "Madama B. F. Pinkerton." For 10 points, identify this opera by Giacomo Puccini in which Cio-Cio San ("cho cho san") commits seppuku.

packet.name: A. NYU A, Tennessee A, Virginia A

tossups.question: Patricia Rozario repopularized opera in this home-country of hers with a 2017 production of Il Matrimonio Segreto at its recently-reopened Royal Opera House. David Murphy premiered the opera and symphony of a musician from this country who collaborated with Philip Glass on the 1990 album Passages. A folktale from this country inspired an opera commemorating Mozart’s 250th birthday that concerns a Prince’s infatuation with a girl who transforms into the title object. This country is the setting of an opera containing a barcarolle for two sopranos that describes a “thick dome” of “white jasmine,” as well as (*) John Adams’s A Flowering Tree. This country’s bayadères (“by-uh-DAIRZ”) title a Ludwig Minkus ballet. This country is home to the conductor of the 1990 Three Tenors concert in Rome who also led the LA Philharmonic in the 1960s and 1970s. In an opera set here, the title princess lures the officer Gérald by singing the Bell Song; that princess also sings the Flower Duet with her servant Mallika. For 10 points, name this setting of Léo Delibes’s (“duh-LEEB’s”) Lakmé, the home of Zubin Mehta and Ravi Shankar.

tossups.question: An operatic title character with this occupation sings the aria “Je t’implore et je tremble” (zhay tam-plore ay zhay trahm-bluh) while preparing to execute a captive. The opera in which that character of this profession appears was written at the height of the composer’s rivalry with Niccolò Piccinni, who wrote an opera on the same subject. A character with this occupation recounts the story of a lowborn daughter who saved a stranger from wild animals by distracting them with the sound of her wand. Another character with this occupation is remembered by the singers of the duet “Au fond du temple saint” (aw fahn duh tahm-pluh sahnt). The glockenspiel echoes one of these characters in the coloratura aria “Où va la jeune Hindoue” (ooh vah zhoon ahn-doo), better known as the “Bell Song.” That character of this profession sings the “Flower Duet” with her servant Mallika and falls in love with the British officer Gérald before committing suicide. For 10 points, what occupation is held by Leila in Bizet’s The Pearl Fishers, and by the title characters of Gluck’s Iphigénie en Tauride and Leo Delibes’s Lakmé?
tossups.answer: <u>priestess</u> (es) [accept more specific answers such as <u>priestess</u> of Brahma or <u>priestess</u> of Artemis]
tossups.answer_sanitized: priestess (es) [accept more specific answers such as priestess of Brahma or priestess of Artemis]

A solo alto saxophone plays the main melody of the “Barcarolle” from Act III of one ballet by this composer, which also features a “pizzicato” divertissement as its most famous movement. In this composer’s most famous operatic selection, which is in B major and 6/8 time, two (*) sopranos sing extended melismas in parallel thirds. Orion kidnaps the title heroine of one ballet by him; Aminta loves that title nymph of Diana. Mallika helps the protagonist gather plants by the riverside in an opera by him, in which the priest Nilankantha forces his daughter to sing “Où va la jeune Hindoue” in order to lure the British soldier Gérald to his death for trespassing on sacred Brahmin grounds. For 10 points, the “Bell Song” and the “Flower Duet” appear in what French composer’s opera Lakmé?

It’s not Lakmé, but this opera sees the title character and her servant sing the Flower Duet while decorating a house. An aria in this opera tells of standing on a hill and predicting the appearance of a (*) white ship. The love duet “Bimba, bimba” is sung in this opera, whose title character also sings “Un bel di” as she awaits the return of her husband in this opera. For 10 points, name this Giacomo Puccini opera set in Japan.

tossups.question: In one of this composer’s operas, the peasant Benoit pretends to be the Marquis de Montcontour’s son in exchange for the hand of the Marquis’s maid Javotte. This composer of La Roit l’a Dit was commissioned to write the “Pas des Fleurs” for Adolphe Adam’s ballet Le Corsaire. He used a bolero castanet rhythm in his setting of de Musset’s poetry, Les Filles de Cadiz, and included a string pizzicato in a ballet about the shepherd Aminta. In another of his ballets, Swanhilde impersonates the giant doll with which Frantz has fallen in love. In one of his operas, the maid Mallika serves the Brahmin Nilakantha, whose daughter sings the “Bell Song.” For 10 points, name this French composer of the ballets Sylvia and Coppelia, who included the “Flower Duet” in his opera Lakme.

parts_sanitized: Name this duet for sopranos from Leo Delibes's Lakme, which is most commonly titled for the objects that Lakme and Malika have gone near a river to obtain.
In the first act of this Georges Bizet opera, the title gypsy throws a flower to the Corporal of the Dragoons, Don Jose ("zho-SAY").

This opera's title character promises the love-struck Narraboth a "little green flower" in exchange for his help. It opens with a rising clarinet scale and the "obsessed" title character's leitmotif, and ends with a "sickening chord" framed by C-sharp major.

bonuses.leadin: In one ballet by this man, Swanhilda shakes wheat by her ear and realizes that Franz no longer loves her when she does not hear it rattle. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this composer, who framed the Sandman story as the ballet Coppelia. He also wrote an opera featuring the Bell Song and the Flower Duet, Lakme.
This other ballet by Delibes takes a classical theme. In it, the title character is a nymph who is captured by Orion but falls in love with Aminta.

Delibes’ opera Lakme is set in this country. Actual dance styles from this country include Kathak, Sattriya, and Chhau, and its characteristic mudras, or hand gestures, are used to communicate through dance.

parts: Name this difficult ballet blanc set to the music of Ludwig Minkus. Its title characters represent the male lead’s opium-induced hallucinations.

This ballet features “The Kingdom of the Shades” after Solor takes opium to console himself after the death of the title character, Nikiya. It was choreographed by Marius Petipa, and premiered in 1877.
La Bayadere takes place in this country, whose perceived exoticism made it a popular setting in the late Romantic Era. Other works set here include Leo Delibes’ opera Lakme.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about Romantic-period opera set near the Indian subcontinent, for 10 points each.
parts: In this composer’s opera The Pearl Fishers, Zurga and Nadir sing the duet “Au fond du temple saint” before having their friendship threatened by a mutual love for Leila.

France [or the French Republic; or le Republique francaise]

In 1952, this British choreographer revived Delibes' ballet Sylvia. He was the principal choreographer of the Royal Ballet from 1933 to 1970, and incorporated traditional English folk dances into his version of La fille mal gardee.

bonuses.leadin: Georges Bizet reworked his incidental music to this novel into two orchestral suites, and it was first published a year after Le Petit Chose as part of Tales from My Windmill. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this work of Alphonse Daudet whose most famous operatic treatment, which sees Frederico's self-defenestration over the cheating title character, was composed by Francesco Cilea.

The title character of Cilea's Adriana Lecouvreur dies after kissing poisoned objects of this type. The title character performs a duet named for these objects with Malika in Delibes's Lakme, while a specific type of this general object makes Octavian the title character in Der Rosenkavalier.

Cilea's works, like Umberto Giordano's Andrea Chenier, are notable examples of this operatic genre often marked by pronounced naturalism. It is most famously exemplified by Mascagni's Cavelleria Rusticana.
answers_sanitized: L'Arlesienne [or L'Arlesiana or The Girl from Arles]
